heading: 9.7.16.9.1.2.Decompression of 6-bit floating point to 8-bit type
content:
A contiguous set of 16 elements of 6-bits each followed by 4 bytes of padding is
decompressed into 16 elements of 8-bits each as shown in [Figure 196](#tcgen05-decompression-6b8b).



[Figure: tcgen05-decompression-6b8b]
CONCEPT BEING ILLUSTRATED:
This figure illustrates the decompression operation from 6-bit packed data format to 8-bit data format, specifically showing how the tcgen05.cp instruction copies and decompresses data from Shared Memory into Tensor Memory on NVIDIA GPUs.

OVERALL LAYOUT:
The figure contains two horizontal memory layout bars arranged vertically, with a downward arrow between them indicating the direction of data flow (copy/decompression operation).

TOP BAR - SHARED MEMORY:
Label: "Shared Memory" (positioned at top-left)
Format label: ".b6x16_p32" (positioned above the bar, roughly centered)
Description: A horizontal bar representing a region of shared memory containing 6-bit packed data. The bar is visually shorter/narrower than the Tensor Memory bar below it, reflecting that 6-bit data is more compact. The bar contains a repeating pattern of colored segments (green, blue/light blue, yellow/tan, pink/salmon, and white segments) separated by thin black dividers. The colored segments appear in groups, representing packed 6-bit values. The rightmost portion of the shared memory bar contains predominantly white/empty-looking segments, which likely represent padding bits associated with the .p32 (pad to 32-bit) format.

ARROW:
A solid blue downward-pointing arrow is positioned between the two bars, centered horizontally.
Label: "tcgen05.cp" (positioned to the right of the arrow)
This indicates the PTX instruction responsible for performing the copy and decompression operation.

BOTTOM BAR - TENSOR MEMORY:
Label: "Tensor Memory" (positioned at bottom-left)
Format label: ".b8x16" (positioned below the bar, roughly centered)
Description: A horizontal bar representing a region of tensor memory containing 8-bit decompressed data. This bar is visually longer/wider than the Shared Memory bar above it, reflecting that 8-bit data occupies more space than 6-bit data. The bar contains a similar repeating pattern of colored segments (green, blue/light blue, yellow/tan, pink/salmon) separated by thin black dividers. The pattern extends further to the right compared to the shared memory bar, and notably there are no white/padding segments — all segments appear to contain valid colored data values.

COLOR PATTERN AND DATA ORGANIZATION:
Both bars use the same color coding to indicate corresponding data elements:
- Green segments
- Light blue/periwinkle segments
- Yellow/tan/gold segments
- Pink/salmon segments
These colors repeat in a consistent cyclic pattern across both bars, allowing visual correspondence between the compressed source data and the decompressed destination data.

NUMERICAL/FORMAT INFORMATION:
- Source format: .b6x16_p32 — meaning 16 elements of 6-bit values, packed with 32-bit padding
- Destination format: .b8x16 — meaning 16 elements of 8-bit values
- Decompression ratio: 6-bit to 8-bit (each 6-bit value is expanded to an 8-bit value)
- The 16 data elements are preserved; only the bit-width per element changes (6 to 8 bits)

DATA FLOW SUMMARY:
Shared Memory (.b6x16_p32) --> [tcgen05.cp instruction] --> Tensor Memory (.b8x16)
The 6-bit packed values (with padding) in shared memory are decompressed/unpacked into full 8-bit values stored in tensor memory. The tensor memory bar is wider than the shared memory bar, visually confirming the expansion in data size from 6-bit to 8-bit representation.


Figure 196 Decompression from 6-bit to 8-bit

The individual 6-bit to 8-bit decompression for types `E3M2` and `E2M3` is shown in
[Figure 197](#tcgen05-decompression-6b8b-individual1) and [Figure 198](#tcgen05-decompression-6b8b-individual2)
respectively.



[Figure: tcgen05-decompression-6b8b-individual1]
This figure illustrates the individual decompression process from a 6-bit E3M2 floating-point format to an 8-bit format.

CONCEPT:
The figure shows how a 6-bit E3M2 floating-point value is expanded/decompressed into an 8-bit representation by zero-extending the upper bits.

LAYOUT DESCRIPTION:

Row 1 - Bit Position Header:
A row of boxes labeled with bit positions from left to right: 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1, 0
These represent the 8 bit positions of the output byte.

Row 2 - 6-bit E3M2 Source Format:
This row is offset to the right, covering only bit positions 5 down to 0 (6 bits total).
The fields are:
- S (Sign bit): occupies bit position 5, colored green/yellow
- E3 (3-bit Exponent): occupies bit positions 4, 3, 2, colored blue/teal
- M2 (2-bit Mantissa): occupies bit positions 1, 0, colored yellow/tan

Row 3 - 8-bit Output Format:
This row spans all 8 bit positions (7 down to 0).
The fields are:
- Bit 7: contains value 0 (fixed zero), colored white/gray
- Bit 6: contains value 0 (fixed zero), colored white/gray
- S (Sign bit): occupies bit position 5, colored green/yellow — same as source
- E3 (3-bit Exponent): occupies bit positions 4, 3, 2, colored blue/teal — same as source
- M2 (2-bit Mantissa): occupies bit positions 1, 0, colored yellow/tan — same as source

DATA FLOW / PATTERN:
The 6-bit E3M2 value (consisting of 1 sign bit + 3 exponent bits + 2 mantissa bits) is placed into the lower 6 bits (positions 5 through 0) of the 8-bit output. The two most significant bits (positions 7 and 6) of the 8-bit output are filled with zeros (zero-extension). The internal field ordering and bit assignments of S, E3, and M2 are preserved exactly during decompression.

SUMMARY OF FIELD MAPPING:
Source 6-bit:  [S][E3][E2][E1][M1][M0]  at positions 5-0
Output 8-bit:  [0][0][S][E3][E2][E1][M1][M0]  at positions 7-0


Figure 197 Individual decompression from 6-bit to 8-bit for E3M2 type



[Figure: tcgen05-decompression-6b8b-individual2]
CONCEPT:
This figure illustrates the bit-level expansion process for decompressing a 6-bit floating point value (E2M3 format) into an 8-bit floating point value. It shows how the individual bit fields are mapped and zero-extended during decompression.

LAYOUT AND STRUCTURE:

The figure contains three horizontal rows, each representing a bit field layout:

ROW 1 - Bit Position Reference Bar (top row):
A row of 8 numbered boxes showing bit positions from left to right:
7 | 6 | 5 | 4 | 3 | 2 | 1 | 0
These are bit index labels (most significant bit 7 on the left, least significant bit 0 on the right).

ROW 2 - 6-bit Source Format (E2M3, middle row):
This row shows the 6-bit input format aligned to the right portion of the 8-bit field (bits 5 down to 0). It contains three colored fields:
- S (Sign bit): 1 bit wide, green/teal background, aligned at bit position 5
- E2 (Exponent, 2 bits): blue/purple background, spanning bit positions 4 and 3
- M3 (Mantissa, 3 bits): yellow/tan background, spanning bit positions 2, 1, and 0

The 6-bit source occupies positions 5 through 0 (the rightmost 6 positions of the 8-bit reference bar). Positions 7 and 6 are not covered by this row (the row starts at bit 5).

ROW 3 - 8-bit Destination Format (bottom row):
This row shows the full 8-bit output format. It contains:
- 0: a zero-filled bit at position 7 (white/empty background)
- 0: a zero-filled bit at position 6 (white/empty background)
- S (Sign bit): 1 bit wide, green/teal background, at bit position 5
- E2 (Exponent, 2 bits): blue/purple background, spanning bit positions 4 and 3
- M3 (Mantissa, 3 bits): yellow/tan background, spanning bit positions 2, 1, and 0

DATA FLOW AND MAPPING:
- The 6-bit E2M3 source value occupies bits [5:0] of the 8-bit output.
- The two most significant bits (positions 7 and 6) of the 8-bit output are filled with zeros (zero-extension).
- The Sign (S), Exponent (E2), and Mantissa (M3) fields are preserved in their relative positions during decompression.
- No bit reordering occurs; the fields map directly from the 6-bit source to the lower 6 bits of the 8-bit destination.

FIELD SUMMARY:
Field | Bits in source | Bits in destination | Color
S     | 1 bit (bit 5)  | 1 bit (bit 5)       | Green/teal
E2    | 2 bits (4-3)   | 2 bits (4-3)        | Blue/purple
M3    | 3 bits (2-0)   | 3 bits (2-0)        | Yellow/tan
Zero  | N/A            | 2 bits (7-6)        | White (value 0)


Figure 198 Individual decompression from 6-bit to 8-bit for E2M3 type
